passages.title: Technology and the Future Workforce in the US
passages.source_html:
Technology and the Future Workforce in the US


A


Work isn’t what it used to be, and in the future it won’t be what it is now. Standardization, mechanization, electrification, and more recently robotification and computerization have driven constant change for more than a century. The changes have been disruptive for many workers as jobs have disappeared, and many communities have suffered long-term decline when their dominant industries withered. In the long run, however, technological progress has been beneficial. As some jobs have been made obsolete, new ones have been created, the economy has adjusted, and society has been better off.


B


But things may be different this time. In earlier times, the transformation of agriculture occurred across a great span of time, giving social systems a longer opportunity to adjust. Now, as the rate of technological advancement is rapidly accelerating, one can only hope that our social systems can become increasingly efficient. If not, the next stage in the evolution of work might prove disastrous for the displaced workers. It seems certain that technological progress will make the economy stronger, but some question its ability to lift all workers and enhance economic equity, while others debate the pace of change. We might find it difficult to provide education that supports not just the privileged, but all members of society. Will traditional and short-term degrees still be able to guarantee a middle class lifestyle? Or will governments have to provide financial backing for ever-more advanced and complex job training, so that workers can keep up with the changes in technology? These are issues on which many scholars have presented differing perspectives.


C


French economist Thomas Piketty looks at the interplay of technological change and larger economic trends in Capital in the 21st Century. He sees advances in technology and the opportunities they provide as forces for social equity. However, he worries that these advances are too weak to offset deeper changes in the structure of the economy, leading to greater concentration of wealth and income for just a few.


D


The labor economist David Autor, of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), thinks that most commentators exaggerate the competition that robots and computers present, and in turn, play down the importance of the unspoken knowledge and integrated skills that people possess. He acknowledges that machines are ideal for ordinary tasks, but he finds that most jobs entail specialized tasks at which humans are superior. He expects workers to enhance their productivity and value by enlisting machines of various types to perform the easy, routine aspects of their jobs, giving them more time to complete the non-routine tasks.


E


Harvard University economist and former treasury secretary Lawrence Summers looks at ongoing changes in the labor market as a sign that trouble is already here.He says that, as a student, he learned and accepted the cheerful view that technology would boost productivity and ultimately benefit workers. But he found it easier to accept that view when the unemployment rate of US males between the ages of 25 and 54 was low, at 6%. At more than double that level and likely to persist, Summers observes that this factor significantly changes the ability of workers to bargain with their employers, and reduces the likelihood that this large group of workers will see their earnings increase. He also asks what makes US productivity growth so sluggish, in spite of this so-called revolution in workplace technology.


F


A more optimistic view comes from Thomas H Davenport and Julia Kirby in their article ‘Beyond Automation’ in Harvard Business Review. Davenport and Kirby follow David Autor’s line of thinking in arguing that technology only enhances human labor, and they develop a variety of strategies by which people can position themselves to benefit from ever more sophisticated mechanization. Unfortunately, the examples they provide refer largely to the highly skilled and educated workers in the top 20% of the wage distribution.


G


The effects of change will differ among industries, across the nature and level of skill, and among demographic groups. Scholar Mark Schneider, for example, explores the changing world of credentials that are becoming alternatives to the typical college experience which takes place over four to eight years. A one-year certificate in a technical field may replace a traditional four-year degree as a sound foundation for a successful career, and the government might step in to support the more extensive and particular educational agendas likely to be in demand.


H


Economics Professor Donna Ginther examines those at the top rung of the education ladder: PhDs in engineering and the fields of physical, life, and social sciences. Their futures have long been quite predictable, but now they are likely to experience some disruption. While an advanced degree was once a fairly reliable guarantee of employment in an academic field, the computer-driven changes in the landscape of higher education have blurred the definition of expertise. There are no longer guarantees. Technology is changing the educational order. Although the pessimists and optimists disagree on how well society will adjust to technology-driven changes in the workplace, they all agree that change is coming and adaptation will be needed.

groups.title: Questions 14-26
groups.source_html:
Questions 14 – 18


Look at the eight sections (A – H). Which section contains the following information?

Question / ParagraphABCDEFG
14

14 a warning about what may happen to the workforce if society doesn’t change sufficiently quickly
✅ 正确答案：B

定位关键词：

society，workforce


原文：

B段, “Now, as the rate of technological advancement is rapidly accelerating, one can only hope that our social systems can become increasingly efficient. If not, the next stage in the evolution of work might prove disastrous for the displaced workers.”


解析：

原文提到随着技术进步的速度迅速加快，人们只能希望社会系统能变得越来越高效。否则，下一阶段的工作演变可能会对失业工人造成灾难性的影响。
15

15 a generalisation about previous patterns of technological developments
✅ 正确答案：A

定位关键词：

technological developments


原文：

A段, “Standardization, mechanization, electrification, and more recently robotification and computerization have driven constant change for more than a century.”


解析：

原文简要概括了一个世纪以来的科技发展进程。
16

16 an observation that the highest educational qualifications no longer ensure job security
✅ 正确答案：H

定位关键词：

the highest educational qualifications 


原文：

H段, “While an advanced degree was once a fairly reliable guarantee of employment in an academic field, the computer-driven changes in the landscape of higher education have blurred the definition of expertise. There are no longer guarantees.”


解析：

原文提到高等学位已经不是学术领域就业的可靠保证。


同义替换：

ensure= guarantees
17

17 a view that shorter training courses might be a substitute for standard university education
✅ 正确答案：G

定位关键词：

shorter training courses，standard university education


原文：

G段, “A one-year certificate in a technical field may replace a traditional four-year degree as a sound foundation for a successful career, and the government might step in to support the more extensive and particular educational agendas likely to be in demand.”


解析：

原文提到一年的技术领域证书可能会取代传统的四年制学位成为成功职业生涯的坚实基础。


同义词替换：

one-year certificate=shorter training courses

traditional four-year degree=standard university education

substitute=replace
18

18 a comparison between the capabilities of technology and those of humans
✅ 正确答案：D

定位关键词：

technology，humans


原文：

D段, “The labor economist David Autor, of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), thinks that most commentators exaggerate the competition that robots and computers present, and in turn, play down the importance of the unspoken knowledge and integrated skills that people possess. He acknowledges that machines are ideal for ordinary tasks, but he finds that most jobs entail specialized tasks at which humans are superior.”


解析：

原文提到David Autor认为机器是普通任务的理想选择，但大多数工作都需要人类擅长的专业任务。

Questions 19 – 22


Choose the correct expert (A – F) for each statement. 


List of Experts

A Thomas Piketty

B David Autor

C Lawrence Summers

D Davenport and Kirby

E Mark Schneider

F Donna Ginther

Options (Word Bank):
A. Thomas Piketty
B. David Autor
C. Lawrence Summers
D. Davenport and Kirby
E. Mark Schneider
F. Donna Ginther
Question / ParagraphABCDEF
19

19 Technology will take power away from employees and prevent salary raises.
✅ 正确答案：C

定位关键词：

prevent salary raises


原文：

E段, “At more than double that level and likely to persist, Summers observes that this factor significantly changes the ability of workers to bargain with their employers, and reduces the likelihood that this large group of workers will see their earnings increase.”


解析：

原文中Lawrence Summers认为技术将使工人受益这一观点显著改变了工人与雇主讨价还价的能力，并降低了大批工人看到他们的收入增加的可能性。
20

20 The capacity of humans is often unrecognised.
✅ 正确答案：B

定位关键词：

capacity of humans，unrecognised


原文：

D段, “The labor economist David Autor, of the Massachusetts Institute of Technology (MIT), thinks that most commentators exaggerate the competition that robots and computers present, and in turn, play down the importance of the unspoken knowledge and integrated skills that people possess.”


解析：

原文中David Autor认为大多数评论家夸大了机器人和计算机带来的竞争，反过来又淡化了人们所拥有知识和综合技能的重要性。
21

21 Previously reliable work for the well-educated is now in doubt.
✅ 正确答案：F

定位关键词：

the well-educated


原文：

H段, “Economics Professor Donna Ginther examines those at the top rung of the education ladder: PhDs in engineering and the fields of physical, life, and social sciences. Their futures have long been quite predictable, but now they are likely to experience some disruption.”


解析：

原文中Donna Ginther调查了处于教育阶梯顶端的人，发现现在他们的未来可能难以预测。


同义词替换：

the top rung of the education ladder=the well-educated
22

22 There are new options for training for the workplace.
✅ 正确答案：E

定位关键词：

new options


原文：

G段, “Scholar Mark Schneider, for example, explores the changing world of credentials that are becoming alternatives to the typical college experience which takes place over four to eight years.”


解析：

原文中Mark Schneider认为一些证书正成为典型四到八年大学经历的替代选择。


同义词替换：

options=alternatives

Questions 23 – 26


Complete the summary. Write ONE WORD ONLY in each gap.

23

Technological Progress


For more than one century, technological innovation such as mechanisation and electrification has continually created major changes for the workforce and weakened a lot of communities for long periods. However, it has also made the economy more productive. While some kinds of work became , others emerged and eventually created stability, sometimes offering better conditions in the long run. In previous centuries, changes in had taken place over a much longer time, and societies had time to adapt. Now, technological improvements are occurring at such a rapid pace that social structures must also become more . Experts have studied different aspects of this problem, such as whether education will only benefit the rich, and whether money from will have to support more specialised careers.

正确答案： 23 obsolete  |  24 agriculture  |  25 efficient  |  26 governments
Question 23

定位关键词：

others emerged，better conditions


原文：

A段, “As some jobs have been made obsolete, new ones have been created, the economy has adjusted, and society has been better off.”


解析：

原文提到随着一些工作被淘汰，新的工作被创造出来，经济进行了调整，社会变得更好了。


同义替换：

emerged=created

Question 24

定位关键词：

changes


原文：

B段, “In earlier times, the transformation of agriculture occurred across a great span of time, giving social systems a longer opportunity to adjust.”


解析：

原文提到曾经农业转型发生在很长一段时间内，给社会制度更长的调整机会。


同义替换：

a much longer time=a great span of time

transformation=changes

Question 25

定位关键词：

technological improvements，social structures


原文：

B段, “Now, as the rate of technological advancement is rapidly accelerating, one can only hope that our social systems can become increasingly efficient.”


解析：

原文提到随着技术进步的速度迅速加快，人们希望社会系统能够变得越来越高效。


同义替换：

social structures= social systems

advancement=improvements

Question 26

定位关键词：

more specialised careers


原文：

B段, “ Or will governments have to provide financial backing for ever-more advanced and complex job training, so that workers can keep up with the changes in technology?”


解析：

原文提到如今很难提供不仅支持特权阶层，而且支持所有社会成员的教育。学者对传统的短期学位能否保证中产阶级的生活方式，以及政府是否必须为日益复杂的职业培训提供财政支持持不同观点。


同义替换：

more specialised=ever-more advanced and complex
